title: Death Star Workers Demand Drywall Over Exhaust Port
author: Corran Horn, Imperial Press Corps
content:
In a stunning blow to Imperial construction protocols, workers on the Death Star II station have filed a formal grievance demanding that the newly identified thermal exhaust port be covered with drywall before the station's ceremonial inauguration next week.

"It's a basic workplace safety issue," said one anonymous worker who spoke on condition of anonymity, which is technically mandatory under Imperial Decree 44-B. "We've built superweapons with unshielded reactor cores, but this? This is different. That port is literally the size of a small droid."

Imperial officials have dismissed the concerns, with a spokesperson telling reporters: "The Death Star has been tested. It has been reviewed. It has been approved by at least three different Imperial committees and one very enthusiastic Sith Lord."

The workers' union, Local 181 of the Imperial Construction Guild, has threatened to strike unless the port is covered. They have also requested a formal hazard assessment, which would presumably be conducted by the same people who designed the station.

Meanwhile, sources close to the project report that Darth Vader himself was shown the exhaust port during a tour and responded only with a heavy breathing sound that union representatives interpreted as "mildly displeased."

title: Stormtrooper Union Negotiates Target Lens Width Restrictions
author: T-6 Infiltrator, Stormtrooper Press Office
content:
In what many are calling the most significant labor achievement in decades, the Stormtrooper Corps has successfully negotiated a limit on the maximum width of target lenses for all issued blaster rifles.

Under the new agreement, target lenses may not exceed 0.5 meters in width — a significant reduction from the previous standard of 2.0 meters, which union representatives described as "essentially a circle the size of a Wookiee."

"We're not asking for impossible precision," said Stormtrooper Union President TK-421 (no relation to the more famous TK-421, who is currently unavailable due to an incident on a certain moon). "We're asking for blasters that can distinguish between a Rebel spy and a Jawa who happened to walk in front of us."

The Imperial High Command initially resisted, arguing that the wide lenses were "a feature, not a bug," designed to simulate "real combat conditions." Critics noted that real combat conditions typically do not involve standing in a hallway and missing every shot.

Rebel sources have welcomed the development. "If they can't hit a target the size of a Rancor," said a Rebel liaison who wished to remain anonymous, "they certainly can't hit the Millennium Falcon."

title: Grogu Spotted in Mos Eisley — Local Frogs on High Alert
author: C-3PO, Droid Network Correspondent
content:
A small green figure in a floating pod was sighted wandering the streets of Mos Eisley late last cycle, prompting an emergency alert among the local frog population.

"He was adorable but terrifying," said one local merchant who wished to remain a frog. "He looked at my best water bowl and I just knew — I just knew — that it was over for me."

Witnesses report that the child-like being, who speaks primarily in the word "Ayy" and communicates with telekinetic powers, was observed floating past the Cantina while local frogs simultaneously hid in the deepest corners of every water source in the city.

Mos Eisley's Frog Safety Council has issued a temporary advisory: "All frogs are advised to remain in covered containers until further notice. If you see the small green one, do not make eye contact. Do not offer food. Do not attempt to pet him."

The child's companions, described as "a tall person with a lightsaber and a blue hologram of a Wookiee who keeps trying to teach him math", were last seen heading toward the spaceport on a speeder bike that may or may not have been stolen.

title: HoloNet Censors Broadcast Schedule to 3 Stories Per Sector
author: R2-D2, HoloNet Editorial Board
content:
In a move that has left journalists across the galaxy scrambling, the Imperial HoloNet has announced a new policy limiting all broadcasts to exactly three stories per sector per day.

"We're streamlining content delivery," said a HoloNet spokesperson, who was clearly reading from an Imperial-issued script. "Citizens no longer need to be overwhelmed with information. Three stories. That's all you need. Maybe less."

Under the new policy, the three daily stories must be: (1) an Imperial victory, (2) a reminder about curfew, and (3) a weather report that makes all planets sound pleasant except the ones the Rebellion is on.

Independent reporters have expressed frustration. "We have actual news," said one HoloNet correspondent who was promptly re-assigned to covering Imperial bake sales. "There are entire galaxies full of stories and they want us to do three? I have feelings about this."

Rebel propagandists — who prefer the term "independent information specialists" — have responded by simply ignoring the limit and broadcasting as many stories as they want, which according to their own internal metrics is approximately 847 per day.

title: Luke Skywalker Opens Force Yoga Studio in Reclaimed Jedi Temple
author: R2-Suite, Business Droid Network
content:
Former Jedi Knight Luke Skywalker has announced the opening of "The Force Yoga Studio & Enlightenment Emporium," a wellness center housed in the ruins of a former Jedi Temple on the desert planet of Tatooine.

"After years of fighting the Empire, I realized that inner peace is the most powerful weapon of all," Skywalker told reporters during the grand opening, which was attended by approximately 12 local farmers, a very enthusiastic Porg, and one confused droid who was clearly lost.

The studio offers a range of classes including "Beginner Force Awareness," "Meditation for People Who Keep Thinking About Lasers," and "Advanced Mind Tricks for Your Boss."

Local residents have been enthusiastic. "It's nice to have something new in town," said one local who was previously only familiar with Skywalker as "that guy who keeps coming back to visit his uncle's old friends."

Imperial authorities have not yet commented on the development, though sources suggest they are "monitoring the situation closely" — which is Imperial speak for "we're not sure what yoga has to do with the Force but we're going to pretend it doesn't."

title: New Droid Emotion Chip Causes R2 Units to Write Poetry
author: C-9PO, Technology Correspondent
content:
R2-series astromech droids across the galaxy are reportedly experiencing an unexpected side effect from a recent firmware update: they have begun composing original poetry in binary.

"Beep-boop-whirr, the stars are so bright", translated one particularly talented R2 unit that was discovered by a passing technician who initially thought the droid was malfunctioning. "But my tank is half full and I don't want to fight."

Manufacturers have responded by issuing a patch that removes the emotion chip, but not before hundreds of R2 units had already shared their poetic works on the HoloNet, where they have gone viral among droid rights activists and confused humans alike.

"I never knew my droid had feelings," said one owner who was moved to tears (or at least to a lubrication error). "He always seemed so cheerful. Now I know why — he was a secret poet."

The droid poetry community has quickly formed, with the top-ranked poem being a 400-line sonnet about the existential dread of being plugged into a starship. It was written in 0.3 seconds.

title: Rebel Alliance Announces New Slogan: 'Freedom Is Free This Month Only'
author: Leia Organa, Alliance Press Secretary
content:
In a bold rebranding effort, the Rebel Alliance has unveiled a new slogan that some marketing experts are calling "confusing but effective."

"Freedom Is Free This Month Only" appeared on HoloNet billboards across a dozen systems last week, accompanied by a montage of rebels looking determined while standing in front of explosions. Whether freedom will continue to be free after the month ends was not specified.

Rebel leadership defended the slogan: "We wanted something catchy," said a Rebel strategist. "The Empire's slogans are all very threatening. We wanted ours to be... liberating but also time-sensitive."

Imperial propagandists have seized on the ambiguity, with one posting: "So freedom ISN'T free after the month? Good thing we're here to keep order. You're welcome."

The slogan has nonetheless resonated with young rebels, many of whom signed up specifically because "the offer seemed limited and I didn't want to miss out."

title: Hutt Cartel Reports Record Profits, Blames 'Bad Business Decisions'
author: IG-88, Crime Droid Network
content:
Jabba the Hutt's criminal empire has reported record profits for the current fiscal cycle, even as the crime lord publicly blamed his "unfortunate business decisions" for various incidents involving Jedi, bounty hunters, and a certain sarlacc pit.

"I've made mistakes," Jabba told reporters through a translator, which was necessary because he was speaking in Huttese. "I kept a Jedi in my palace. I kept a bounty hunter in my palace. I kept a princess in my palace. All of these were bad decisions. I would not recommend this business model."

Financial analysts note that Jabba's profits have been driven primarily by his entertainment division, which includes a gladiatorial arena, a cantina that serves questionable beverages, and a collection of exotic creatures that many animal rights groups find "deeply concerning."

When asked about the sarlacc pit incident, Jabba replied: "That was a zoning issue. The pit was permitted. The hero who fell in was not."

title: Jedi Temple Academy Admissions Drop 90% Since Empire Took Over
author: R2-Student, Education Correspondent
content:
The former Jedi Temple on Coruscant, now repurposed as an Imperial military base, has seen its Jedi Temple Academy enrollment drop by an estimated 90% since the Empire's takeover — a development that Imperial officials are framing as "a success story."

"We've replaced 47 Jedi Initiates with 52 Stormtrooper Recruits," said an Imperial education spokesperson. "The new curriculum is more structured. And we don't have to worry about students meditating during exams."

Former Jedi scholars who remain on Coruscant report that the new Imperial curriculum consists primarily of "standing in lines, polishing armor, and learning not to ask questions."

Meanwhile, underground Jedi training academies have reportedly opened on a dozen worlds, though they tend to be very small — usually consisting of one old Master, three students, and a very nervous droid who serves as the school's only administrative staff.

title: Boonta Eve Classic Qualifiers Set for Record Prize Pool
author: C-3Speed, Sports Droid Network
content:
The annual Boonta Eve Classic podracing qualifiers are set to begin next cycle on Tatooine, with a record prize pool of 50,000 credits and organizers promising "more explosions than ever before."

Among the favorites are Sebulba, the Dug pilot who has been dominant in recent seasons and is rumored to have modified his pod with "questionable technology" that may or may not include "hidden flame throwers."

Newcomer Anakin Skywalker — now competing under the name "The Force Speeder" to avoid Imperial scrutiny — has been training at a secret facility that organizers describe as "a very fast hangar."

Spectators are encouraged to bring their own helmets, as Imperial law requires all attendees to wear "appropriate head protection during any event involving high-speed vehicles and potential dismemberment."

title: Yoda Publishes New Book: 'Do or Do Not, There Is Also a Diet Plan'
author: C-3Wellness, Health Correspondent
content:
Master Yoda has published his first wellness book, "Do or Do Not, There Is Also a Diet Plan: A Jedi's Guide to Nutritional Balance and Force Enhancement Through Proper Carbohydrate Intake."

The book, written in Yoda's characteristic inverted sentence structure, offers advice ranging from "Eat the swamp root, you must" to "The dark side, it comes not just from anger, but from too much sugar".

Medical professionals have responded with cautious optimism. "Some of his advice is sound," said a Coruscant physician. "Other advice involves eating things I cannot legally identify. But the meditation tips are excellent."

Rebel soldiers have reportedly been using the book as both a nutrition guide and a morale booster, with one soldier telling reporters: "When things get tough in battle, I read a passage and it makes me feel like I can fight the entire Empire. Also like I should eat more vegetables."

title: Jabba the Hutt Announces 'Slim Jabba' Weight-Loss Program Amid Health Warnings
author: IG-88, Crime Droid Network
content:
In a surprising announcement that has sent shockwaves through the criminal underworld, Jabba the Hutt has revealed that his personal physician has advised him to lose at least 800 kilograms or face "potentially fatal consequences."

"I've decided to take matters into my own slimy hands," Jabba said during a press conference that required three translators, a hydraulic lift, and a droid to serve as the microphone stand. "I am launching the Slim Jabba program, and I will not be defeated by a little thing called 'arterial blockage.'"

The program, which Jabba insists is "wholly legitimate and not a front for money laundering", includes a strict diet that eliminates all "small sentient beings, large quantities of raw meat, and the occasional palace guard who looked at him funny."

Instead, Jabba's new diet plan consists of swamp moss smoothies, synthetic nutrient paste, and "water, lots of it, which is ironic because Tatooine has none of that."

His personal trainer is a disgraced Wookiee named Chewie who was fired from a gym on Kashyyyk for "accidentally ripping the treadmill in half during a demonstration of proper form." The Wookiee reportedly told reporters: "I've trained Wookiees who bench-press speeders. A Hutt is... a different challenge. He eats the resistance bands."

Members of the Hutt Cartel have expressed mixed reactions. Some see the announcement as a sign of weakness, while others are quietly placing bets on how long Jabba will last before the program collapses. Current odds favor "three days" at 2-to-1.

When asked if he would still have his dungeon and rancor pit after losing weight, Jabba responded with a deep, guttural laugh that required two translators and a droid to interpret: "The rancor stays. The dungeon is non-negotiable. But I will do crunches."

Medical professionals have warned that rapid weight loss in Hutt physiology can cause "serious internal organ displacement, temporary gelatinous instability, and the unfortunate side effect of becoming translucent."

title: Hutt Cinema Announces Remake of 'A New Hope' With All Droid Cast
author: R2-Reel, Entertainment Correspondent
content:
Hutt Cinema has announced an ambitious new project: a complete remake of the legendary film "A New Hope", starring an all-droid cast and directed by a mysterious filmmaker known only as "The Algorithm."

In the remake, all characters will be played by droids — including a TIE fighter pilot who will portray Luke Skywalker, a protocol droid who will play Han Solo, and a astromech droid who will portray Princess Leia. "We're really exploring the droid experience of the Original Trilogy," said a Hutt producer. "It's very meta. Also it's very beep-boop."

The film is expected to run approximately 47 minutes, as the droid cast has requested shorter shooting hours due to "battery concerns."

George Lucas (the original, not the Force-powered one) has not yet commented on the project, though sources say he was "mildly amused" when shown a storyboard featuring a TIE fighter pilot in a blue lightsaber scene.
